name: Expense page orchestration refactor
description: Refactor the expense feed and expense detail route surfaces to better match the frontend architecture guidance: keep page orchestration thin, move pure filter logic into focused helpers with unit coverage, adopt the shared PageShell route pattern, and consolidate repeated async fallback UI without changing expense behavior.

evidence.description: Expenses page now uses the shared PageShell route pattern, keeps route-surface composition focused on top-level orchestration, and delegates filter/category/group derivation to a dedicated helper module.
Expense detail page now renders loading, not-found, forbidden, error, and success states inside one PageShell and reuses shared fallback building blocks instead of duplicating state markup.
Pure expense-feed helpers now own category filtering, group deduplication, API filter building, and active-filter label generation so the page file stays smaller and easier to review.
Focused Vitest coverage locks the extracted helper behavior for categories, group merging, API filter conversion, and active-filter labels without adding forbidden component render tests.
Focused web helper test passed with 4 assertions after adjusting the expectations to the canonical amount parser semantics.
Full repository verification passed after the expense-page refactor and harness updates.
Final GitNexus change detection reported LOW risk with no affected execution flows.
